questions.question: Who is allowed to initially set up the GitHub AI Connector for a Notion workspace?

options.text: Any member of the Notion workspace
options.explanation: Only workspace owners can perform the initial setup, not any member.

options.text: A GitHub organization owner who is also the Notion workspace owner
options.explanation: The connector requires both the GitHub organization owner and the Notion workspace owner to set it up.

options.text: Only the Notion workspace owner, regardless of GitHub role
options.explanation: Both roles are required; being just a Notion owner isn’t sufficient.

options.text: Any user with a Business or Enterprise plan
options.explanation: Plan eligibility alone isn’t enough; ownership of both the workspace and GitHub organization is needed.

questions.question: Which of the following types of GitHub content are NOT supported by the connector as of now?

options.text: Public repositories
options.explanation: Public repositories are supported and require no further authentication after setup.

options.text: Private repositories after individual authentication
options.explanation: Private repositories are supported once each user logs in with GitHub.

options.text: Wikis attached to a repository
options.explanation: The article explicitly states that wikis are not supported.

options.text: README files
options.explanation: README files are indexed with no time restriction.

questions.question: After the initial connector setup, how often does Notion AI index new GitHub data?

options.text: Every 30 minutes
options.explanation: The article notes that new content is indexed every 30 minutes after the initial setup.

options.text: Every hour
options.explanation: Permissions are synced hourly, but data indexing occurs every 30 minutes.

options.text: Once daily
options.explanation: The frequency is more frequent than once per day.

options.text: Only when a user manually triggers a refresh
options.explanation: Indexing happens automatically, not only on manual refresh.

questions.question: If a Notion workspace member wants to access private repositories after an owner has set up the connector, what must the member do?

options.text: Request the workspace owner to grant them additional Notion permissions
options.explanation: Permissions are derived from GitHub, not additional Notion permissions.

options.text: Log in to GitHub via the Notion AI interface to authenticate their personal GitHub account
options.explanation: Each member must individually authenticate with GitHub to access private repos.

options.text: Wait for the 72‑hour initial setup window to finish
options.explanation: Authentication is required after the initial setup, regardless of the 72‑hour window.

options.text: Create a new GitHub organization and move the repos there
options.explanation: Creating a new organization is unnecessary for accessing existing private repos.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about the time range of searchable PRs and issues is correct?

options.text: Only the most recent 30 days of PRs and issues are indexed
options.explanation: The connector indexes up to one year of PRs and issues.

options.text: All historical PRs and issues are available without limit
options.explanation: There is a one‑year limit for PRs and issues.

options.text: Up to one year’s worth of PRs and issues can be retrieved
options.explanation: The article states that Notion AI can retrieve up to a year’s worth of PRs and issues.

options.text: Only PRs from the last 6 months are searchable; issues have no limit
options.explanation: Both PRs and issues share the same one‑year limit.

questions.question: Where can a user invoke the GitHub AI Connector within Notion? Select the answer that lists ALL available locations.

options.text: Only in the Notion AI chat at the bottom right of the page
options.explanation: There are additional locations besides the bottom‑right chat.

options.text: In the Notion AI widget at the top of the Home page and the sidebar search
options.explanation: The bottom‑right chat is also supported.

options.text: In the Notion AI chat (bottom right), the AI widget at the top of Home, and the sidebar search
options.explanation: All three locations are mentioned as places where the connector can be used.

options.text: Only via the Notion AI widget on the Home page
options.explanation: This omits two other supported entry points.

questions.question: If a user tries to query a private repository they do not have access to on GitHub, what will Notion AI do?

options.text: Return a generic error stating the repository is unavailable
options.explanation: The connector silently omits results rather than showing an error.

options.text: Return no results for that repository, respecting the user’s GitHub permissions
options.explanation: Permissions are mapped from GitHub; if a user lacks access, Notion AI will not return data.

options.text: Prompt the user to request access from the repository owner
options.explanation: The system does not automatically request access; it simply provides no data.

options.text: Show the repository’s public README but hide the code
options.explanation: If the repo is private, even the README is hidden unless the user has permission.

questions.question: What is the recommended option when selecting repositories during the initial GitHub integration setup?

options.text: Select only the repositories you plan to query frequently
options.explanation: While possible, the article recommends selecting all repositories.

options.text: Select All repositories (recommended)
options.explanation: The guide explicitly recommends choosing “All repositories” during setup.

options.text: Select only public repositories and ignore private ones
options.explanation: Private repositories can be accessed after individual authentication.

options.text: Select no repositories and add them later manually
options.explanation: At least one repository must be selected during the initial connection.

questions.question: How does Notion store the data it indexes from GitHub for AI queries?

options.text: It stores raw files directly in Notion’s relational database
options.explanation: Raw files are not stored; embeddings are used instead.

options.text: It creates embeddings and stores them in a vector database hosted by Turbopuffer
options.explanation: The article explains that embeddings are stored in Turbopuffer’s vector DB.

options.text: It caches the content in the user’s browser local storage
options.explanation: Data is stored server‑side, not in the browser.

options.text: It forwards the content to a third‑party AI model each time a query is made without persisting it
options.explanation: Persistent embeddings are created for efficient retrieval.

questions.question: If a workspace owner connects the wrong Notion workspace to a GitHub organization, what steps can they take to correct it?

options.text: Delete the GitHub organization and recreate it under the correct workspace
options.explanation: Deleting the organization is unnecessary and extreme.

options.text: Go to Settings → Notion AI, click the gear icon next to GitHub, then choose Disconnect
options.explanation: The article provides these exact steps for disconnecting or editing the integration.

options.text: Contact Notion support to request a manual reset
options.explanation: While support can help, the UI provides a direct disconnect option.

options.text: Log out of both Notion and GitHub, then log back in to refresh the connection
options.explanation: Logging out does not change the workspace that was linked.
